title: Bio-optical Properties of Cultured Phytoplankton Over Complete Diel Cycles
abstract: Laboratory culture study measuring bio-optical and physiological properties (absorption spectra, pigment composition, particulate organic carbon, growth rates) of phytoplankton cultures sampled across complete diel (24-hour) cycles, supporting a manuscript on diel variability in phytoplankton optics.

resources.name: Final bottle-level and experiment-level summary data

variables.name: gaussian_decompositions
variables.label: Gaussian Decomposition Components
variables.description: Component Gaussian functions fit to unsmoothed and decomposed absorption spectra.

variables.name: pigment_ratios
variables.label: Pigment Absorption Ratios
variables.description: Peaks of individual pigment absorption Gaussian functions and ratios between them.

variables.name: correlations
variables.label: Spearman Correlation Coefficients
variables.description: Nonparametric Spearman correlations between phytoplankton physiological measurements and bio-optical properties at 470/550/660 nm.

access.how_to_request: Mirrored from a public GitHub repository accompanying the manuscript; freely available per the repository README.

provenance.originator: Baetge, N., Halesy, K., Graff, J.R., Ver Wey, B., Westberry, T., Appel, A.E., Bourdin, G., Begouen Demeaux, C., Boss, E., Behrenfeld, M.J.
provenance.license: Freely available for use per repository README
provenance.citation: Baetge, N. et al. (in review). Bio-optical properties of cultured phytoplankton over complete diel cycles.

known_issues: Not Red-Sea-specific — a general phytoplankton physiology reference study, included for its bio-optical methodology relevance rather than basin coverage.
